disputed_language: An identity in a member account can use a permission only when every SCP in the path from the organization root through the account permits it, in addition to applicable IAM and resource policies.
explanation: The claim conflates every hierarchy level with every individual SCP. AWS requires an explicit Allow at each level, but multiple SCPs attached at one level are evaluated collectively; any applicable explicit Deny can block access. The wording also presents IAM/resource policies as if they complete the effective-permission model while current AWS documentation explicitly includes resource control policies and other policy evaluation constraints.
required_action: Replace the claim with one or more atomic statements that distinguish allow-at-every-level from explicit-deny behavior and accurately bound the other applicable policy types; cite the SCP evaluation section precisely.
suggested_revision: For a member account, an action must be allowed at every level from the root through each OU and the account, and no applicable SCP may explicitly deny it; final authorization also depends on the applicable IAM, resource, RCP, boundary, and session-policy evaluation.

supporting_source.locator: How SCPs work with Allow; How SCPs work with Deny, especially the every-level rule and any-SCP deny rule
supporting_source.title: SCP evaluation
